explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.findings: Published materials reference engagement with a third party on responsible AI.
external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Publish third-party audit results, certifications, or regulatory compliance documentation.

relevant_sources.summary: This article from Sarasin & Partners details their engagement with ServiceNow on responsible AI, providing evidence for **external_accountability, fairness, governance, oversight, privacy, and transparency**. The article highlights ServiceNow's publication of Human-Centred Responsible AI Guidelines and their governance approach, which includes a shared responsibility model with customer participation in AI risk assessment and mitigation through mechanisms like model cards and human oversight systems. This demonstrates a commitment to transparency regarding AI risks, fairness by addressing bias, and robust governance and oversight structures.
relevant_sources.title: Shaping the future of ethical AI - Sarasin & Partners

relevant_sources.summary: This press release from ServiceNow details the company's commitment to responsible AI, providing evidence for **transparency**, **fairness**, **governance**, and **oversight**. The article highlights ServiceNow's approach to making AI understandable to users, ensuring AI models are trained and tested to reflect a diverse user base, and establishing internal committees and councils to review AI use cases. Furthermore, the press release mentions a "human-in-the-loop" process for AI evaluation and the use of in-product feedback to monitor and refine AI usefulness, demonstrating mechanisms for oversight and governance.
relevant_sources.title: ServiceNow's 4 key AI principles and why they matter to your business

fairness.findings: ServiceNow publishes Human-Centred Responsible AI Guidelines. Documentation describes ensuring AI models are trained and tested to reflect a diverse user base.
fairness.path_to_improvement: Publish bias testing results, outcome monitoring, or vendor fairness certifications.

governance.findings: Documentation describes a governance approach that includes a shared responsibility model with customer participation in AI risk assessment and mitigation. It also describes establishing internal committees and councils to review AI use cases. Furthermore, documentation discusses the operationalization of AI governance and risk management, outlining the identification, evaluation, and mitigation of AI risks, and details a security guardrail to prevent prompt injection attacks.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

The article highlights ServiceNow's approach to making AI understandable to users, ensuring AI models are trained and tested to reflect a diverse user base, and establishing internal committees and councils to review AI use cases. relevant_sources.summary: This company-owned webinar, "Getting started with AI Risk Management and Governance on the ServiceNow AI Platform," provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. The webinar supports governance by discussing the operationalization of AI governance and risk management, including identifying, evaluating, and mitigating AI risks. It also indicates planned AI system capabilities and integration governance through mentions of AI agents and interoperability, which are key aspects of transparency in how AI systems are designed and deployed.
relevant_sources.title: Getting started with AI Risk Management and Governance on the ServiceNow AI Platform

relevant_sources.summary: This company-owned help page, "Now Assist Guardian FAQ," provides evidence for **governance** and **transparency**. It supports governance by detailing the security guardrail designed to prevent prompt injection attacks, indicating a mechanism for controlling AI behavior. Transparency is supported by the FAQ's explanation of how the system detects and responds to malicious inputs, implying openness about the AI's security capabilities and potential actions.
relevant_sources.title: Now Assist Guardian FAQ

oversight.findings: Documentation specifies human oversight systems and references a "human-in-the-loop" process for AI evaluation. It also references the use of in-product feedback to monitor and refine AI usefulness.
oversight.path_to_improvement: Publish override mechanisms, escalation processes, or appeal workflows.

privacy.path_to_improvement: Publish AI-specific privacy impact assessments or data handling procedures.

transparency.findings: ServiceNow publishes Human-Centred Responsible AI Guidelines and describes an approach to making AI understandable to users. Documentation specifies the use of model cards and describes a shared responsibility model with customer participation in AI risk assessment and mitigation. Furthermore, documentation references planned AI system capabilities and explains how the system detects and responds to malicious inputs.
transparency.path_to_improvement: Publish detailed documentation (model cards, system specs) for AI systems deployed.

summary.overall_findings: ServiceNow's governance approach includes a shared responsibility model with customer participation in AI risk assessment and mitigation, while its transparency materials reference Human-Centred Responsible AI Guidelines. These are among the 6 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars for which documented evidence was found, with all six covered pillars addressed at the policy level. Fairness disclosures describe ensuring AI models are trained and tested to reflect a diverse user base, and oversight documentation specifies human oversight systems. No qualifying public evidence was found for explainability, with the assessment drawing on 22 publicly available sources.
